work_package_result.summary: Defined additive provider-neutral contracts for inspecting, registering and rebinding project folders; stable project identity and relocatable workspace bindings; optional portable .dennett identity and shared memory; local-only trust policy; workspace snapshots, changes, commands, tests, artifacts, checkpoints and review; and immutable cancellation/recovery receipts. Existing folders without .dennett are detected and can only receive the minimal portable structure after an explicit user choice.

passed: 17 protocol generator tests cover the accepted M01 surface, the complete initial M02 descriptor, exact enums and oneofs, trust decision and compare-and-swap fields, deprecations, explicit portable actions, cancellation identity and negative compatibility probes

7 dennett-contracts tests cover stable wire names, safe Unicode relative paths, nonzero revisions, fail-closed trust, existing-project policy preservation, legal receipt matrices and immutable terminal cancellation

mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/protocol_codegen.py check passed Buf lint, generated-client freshness, the full descriptor contract, origin/main compatibility and negative breaking probes
the previously contended concurrent lifecycle test passed alone and again inside the full workspace run
mise exec -- just check passed repository, documentation, planning, CI, formatting, clippy, Rust workspace tests, Python tests, TypeScript type checks and protocol checks
not_run: 2 live subscription tests remain ignored by the generic full gate because this contract-only package does not change the already accepted Codex runtime path
actual folder inspection, .dennett creation, durable registration, trust enforcement, Git behavior and desktop interaction belong to WP-M02-003 and later packages
provider adapters other than Codex and local models remain unavailable by owner constraint

review.method: Two detached adversarial reviewers independently audited architecture and wire semantics. Their findings were repaired and rechecked; the final architecture and protocol reviews reported no remaining P0-P2 findings.
findings_closed: made trust elevation depend on an explicit Trust-issued decision and expected policy revision instead of accepting model or folder claims
preserved existing local policy when a portable ProjectId is already registered and made copied identity resolution an explicit registration-only fork
deprecated the legacy existing-folder shortcut so non-empty folders cannot bypass .dennett inspection and registration
separated cancellation-command identity from its target operation and made every already-terminal receipt, including recovery-required, an immutable idempotent no-op
defined exhaustive legal receipt state and outcome matrices and removed contradictory test outcome states
pinned the complete first M02 descriptor so later field, enum, oneof, option or method drift is detected before release

migrations: none; this package adds a pre-release protocol surface and generated clients without creating durable user data
observability_changes: workspace mutations retain command, correlation and one canonical workspace operation identity
typed terminal failures distinguish stale revision, missing location, denied scope, conflict, cancellation, retryable adapter failure and recovery-required state
snapshots and receipts expose stable revisions and timestamps without provider-specific or filesystem implementation payloads
known_limitations: this package defines and validates contracts only; it does not yet inspect folders or write the minimal .dennett structure
the desktop offer to create .dennett becomes executable only after WP-M02-003 owns registration and trust behavior
workspace snapshot history lists need a bounded window or pagination before long-lived production histories
Codex remains the only permitted real runtime until the owner enables additional providers
follow_up_candidates: WP-M02-003 implement durable project registration, existing-folder inspection, explicit minimal .dennett creation and local trust policy
add bounded snapshot history pagination before long-lived workspace histories
connect later filesystem, Git, test, artifact and review adapters through the provider-neutral contracts
